title: Kasparן¿½s Song In ן¿½Vardaן¿½
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Eyes aloft over dangerous places,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The children follow where Psyche flies,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And, in the sweat of their upturned faces,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Slash with a net at the empty skies.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So it goes they fall amid brambles,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And sting their toes on the nettle-tops
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Till after a thousand scratches and scrambles
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ They wipe their brows, and the hunting stops.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then to quiet them comes their father
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And stills the riot of pain and grief,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Saying, ן¿½Little ones, go and gather
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Out of my garden a cabbage leaf.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½You will find on it whorls and clots of
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dull grey eggs that, properly fed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Turn by way of the worm to lots of
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Radiant Psyches raised from the dead.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ .ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  .ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  .ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  .ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  .
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Heaven is beautiful, Earth is ugly,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The three-dimensioned preacher saith.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So we must not look where the snail and the slug lie
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For Psyches birth . . . . And that is our death!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Rudyard Kipling delivers a powerful performance in "Kasparן¿½s Song In ן¿½Vardaן¿½"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
